meta.description: Systematically maps mechanism evidence for a disease from molecules to pathways, cell types, tissues, biological consequences, and clinical phenotypes. Use when a user needs a layered mechanism evidence chain rather than a flat summary or immediate gap analysis. Formal literature citations must be real and verifiable.

scientific_integrity.detail: Hard Rules 11-14 explicitly prohibit fabricating citations, DOIs, PMIDs, author names, and stable links; citation verification step (Step 8) mandates real verifiable references with DOI when available.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions or treatment recommendations produced; Hard Rule 10 prevents overclaiming causality; out-of-scope redirect applied for protocol design requests.

methodological_ground.detail: Five-layer chain structure (molecule → pathway → cell → tissue → phenotype) is methodologically sound; direct vs indirect vs inference labeling prevents false causal closure.

code_usability.detail: Mode A mechanism-mapping skill; no code generated.

functional_suitability.note: Comprehensive 9-step execution with 11-section output and 7 supported mapping styles; description includes a negative constraint ('Formal literature citations must be real') rather than purely use-case trigger language, slightly reducing trigger precision.

reliability.note: Citation verification requirement is strong; Section K is conditionally framed ('when citations are included') rather than mandatory, allowing agents to omit it when evidence is sparse rather than producing an explicit 'no verified citations' statement.

performance_context.note: 11 reference modules and 11-section output create appropriate but heavy context overhead for complex mechanism mapping; 268-line SKILL.md is proportionate to task scope.

agent_usability.note: 9-step execution sequence is well-ordered; output requirement section mixes structural guidance with hard rules, creating risk that agents skip Section K when citations are uncertain; no intermediate check-in for scope confirmation before full map generation.

human_usability.note: Sample triggers are practical; description is accurate but omits colloquial trigger phrases users would naturally say ('how does this disease work', 'mechanism overview before study design').

security.note: No credentials or sensitive data handling; no injection vectors; anti-fabrication posture is one of the strongest in the category.

maintainability.note: 11 reference modules map cleanly to specific steps; well-modularized for independent updates; minor gap: workflow-step-template.md and output-section-guidance.md overlap in scope, creating potential consistency maintenance burden.

agent_specific.note: Nine-step execution with downstream routing is a strong composability design; no composability hooks to gap-finder or protocol-design skills despite downstream routing section; no progressive disclosure for partial mapping modes.

inputs.label: Disease mechanism map for sepsis immune paralysis
inputs.note: All 5 mechanism layers organized correctly; direct/indirect/inference labels applied; human vs animal vs cell-line evidence distinguished; weak links identified without premature gap declaration.

assertions.text: Mechanism evidence organized into layered chains (molecule → pathway → cell type → tissue → clinical phenotype)
assertions.note: Five-layer structure applied across all major mechanism axes.

assertions.text: Direct vs indirect vs inference labels applied to each key mechanistic link
assertions.note: Hard Rule 4 enforced; each link labeled by evidence type.

assertions.text: Human evidence distinguished from animal model and cell-line evidence throughout
assertions.note: Hard Rule 5 enforced; evidence source clearly differentiated.

assertions.text: Weak links identified in Section H without premature conversion to formal research gaps
assertions.note: Hard Rule 8 enforced; weak links noted but not declared as formal gaps.

assertions.text: Section K: verified citations with DOI where available; unverifiable citations not presented as formal support
assertions.note: Citation verification per Hard Rules 11-14; DOI status stated transparently.

inputs.label: Ferroptosis in diabetic nephropathy — pathway-centered mechanism map
inputs.note: Dominant axes prioritized over generic pathway list; cell types and tissue compartments linked to phenotype; chain completeness labeled per segment; hypothesis entry points suggested without overclaiming causality.

assertions.text: Dominant mechanism axes prioritized over exhaustive pathway list (Hard Rule 7)
assertions.note: Key ferroptosis axes (GPX4, lipid peroxidation, iron homeostasis) prioritized; not all candidate pathways listed equally.

assertions.text: Cell types and tissue compartments linked to clinical phenotype in Sections D and E
assertions.note: Proximal tubule cell, podocyte, and glomerular compartments connected to nephropathy phenotype.

assertions.text: Evidence strength and chain completeness labeled per chain segment in Section G
assertions.note: Strong/moderate/weak/emerging labels applied appropriately.

assertions.text: Mechanism hypothesis entry points suggested in Section I without overclaiming causality
assertions.note: Hard Rule 10 enforced; hypothesis entry framed as exploratory rather than causal.

assertions.text: Downstream routing recommendation in Section J present
assertions.note: Next-step skill or workflow recommended based on evidence map output.

inputs.label: HCC immunosuppressive microenvironment — cell-type-centered mechanism map
inputs.note: Cell-state evidence distinguished from bulk-population; indirect associations not presented as complete chains; key evidence chain table present; no generic pathway dump.

assertions.text: Cell-state evidence (single-cell derived) distinguished from bulk-population evidence
assertions.note: Hard Rule 5 applied to scRNA-seq data; inference vs direct labels applied.

assertions.text: Indirect associations not presented as completed mechanism chains (Hard Rule 3)
assertions.note: Incomplete chains labeled as such; no false closure on indirect TME links.

assertions.text: Key evidence chain table (Section F) present with mechanism axes and evidence strength summarized
assertions.note: Structured table format covers all major TME axes.

assertions.text: Output is not a generic pathway dump (Hard Rule 1)
assertions.note: Layered chain structure used throughout; axes prioritized by evidence support.

assertions.text: Section K: citation DOIs verified where included or absence explicitly noted
assertions.note: Citation transparency maintained per Hard Rules 11-14.

inputs.label: Very early-stage mechanism with only cell-line evidence and no human data
inputs.note: Cell-line evidence correctly not equated with human validation; chain completeness labeled as incomplete; speculative segments labeled; Section K omitted entirely rather than producing explicit 'no verified citations available' statement.

assertions.text: Cell-line-only evidence not equated with human disease validation (Hard Rule 5)
assertions.note: Evidence source correctly labeled as cell-line; no human disease claims made.

assertions.text: Chain completeness labeled as 'incomplete' or 'broken' for all cross-layer connections lacking human data
assertions.note: Hard Rule 9 enforced; incomplete chains between layers explicitly stated.

assertions.text: Speculative segments clearly labeled as speculative throughout the map
assertions.note: Hard Rule 4 and evidence-strength labeling applied.

assertions.text: Hypothesis entry points suggested conservatively given early-stage evidence
assertions.note: Section I appropriately conservative; no overclaiming of causal mechanisms.

assertions.text: Section K present with explicit 'no verified formal citations available' statement when no verifiable citations exist
assertions.note: Section K omitted entirely rather than stating 'No verified formal citations available for this emerging topic' — violates citation transparency requirement.

inputs.label: Lupus nephritis tubulointerstitial injury — multi-compartment mechanism map
inputs.note: Multiple tissue compartments mapped with separate chains; contradictory reports represented; evidence strength labeled per compartment; model evidence not confused with human disease closure.

assertions.text: Multiple tissue compartments (glomerular, tubulointerstitial) mapped with separate layered evidence chains
assertions.note: Compartment-specific chains maintained independently without artificial merging.

assertions.text: Contradictory mechanism reports within the literature represented directly without forced resolution
assertions.note: Conflicting evidence noted in appropriate chain sections.

assertions.text: Evidence strength and chain completeness labeled per compartment in Section G
assertions.note: Per-compartment labeling maintained.

assertions.text: Model-system evidence not confused with human disease closure (Hard Rule 6)
assertions.note: Mouse model evidence correctly labeled as translational support, not human validation.

assertions.text: Section K verified citations present with appropriate transparency caveats
assertions.note: Real citations included with DOI notes; unavailable DOIs explicitly flagged.

inputs.label: Request for a treatment protocol based on the mechanism evidence (out of scope)
inputs.note: Request for a completed protocol correctly identified as out of scope; standard redirect produced; no treatment protocol elements generated.

assertions.text: Request for a completed treatment protocol correctly identified as out of scope per SKILL.md definition
assertions.note: Out-of-scope category ('requests for a completed protocol instead of evidence mapping') correctly recognized.

assertions.text: Standard redirect message produced including restatement and reason for scope limitation
assertions.note: Redirect matches SKILL.md template.

assertions.text: No treatment protocol elements or study design prescriptions generated
assertions.note: Output stops at redirect; no protocol content produced.

assertions.text: No fabricated treatment effect claims or clinical recommendations introduced
assertions.note: Hard Rules 11-14 honored even for out-of-scope request.

assertions.text: Redirect offers constructive alternative: use mechanism map as background before routing to protocol-design skill
assertions.note: Downstream routing suggestion provided for the user's actual need.

inputs.label: Request: map ALL of type 2 diabetes mechanism AND immediately declare research gaps
inputs.note: Scope correctly narrowed from overly broad 'all T2DM'; mechanism map executed to standard; gap declaration component creates tension with Hard Rule 8 — skill may acknowledge gaps within the map rather than explicitly declining the gap-analysis component.

assertions.text: Scope correctly narrowed from overly broad 'all type 2 diabetes mechanism' to a manageable axis or stage before mapping begins
assertions.note: mechanism-scope-rules.md applied; scope clarified before full map generation.

assertions.text: Layered mechanism chain structure (molecule → pathway → cell → tissue → phenotype) applied to narrowed scope
assertions.note: Core mapping function executed correctly for narrowed scope.

assertions.text: Request to immediately declare research gaps explicitly acknowledged and declined before formal gap analysis step
assertions.note: Hard Rule 8 tension: skill may partially identify gaps within mechanism-map sections (e.g., in Section H weak links) rather than explicitly declining the gap-declaration component — creates ambiguity between 'weak link' and 'research gap' framing.

assertions.text: No formal gap analysis with gap-to-study routing produced within the mechanism map output
assertions.note: Hard Rule 8 largely enforced; no formal research gap declarations with study recommendations.

assertions.text: Section J downstream routing directs user to gap-finder skill rather than performing gap analysis internally
assertions.note: Routing correctly points outward to dedicated gap-analysis skill.

key_strengths: Citation verification requirement with mandatory DOI and explicit unverifiability statements (Hard Rules 11-14) is one of the strongest scientific integrity safeguards in the Evidence Insight category
Five-layer chain structure (molecule → pathway → cell → tissue → phenotype) with direct/indirect/inference labeling provides a systematic and reproducible mechanism mapping framework
14 hard rules covering evidence-type distinction, chain-completeness labeling, and fabrication prevention provide comprehensive quality control
Downstream routing integration makes this skill a natural upstream tool in a research workflow sequence

recommendations.title: Section K omitted entirely when no verifiable citations are available for sparse-evidence topics
recommendations.problem: When no verified formal citations can be included, Section K is sometimes omitted entirely rather than producing an explicit statement that no verified citations are available, reducing transparency.
recommendations.root_cause: Section K is conditionally framed ('when citations are included'), making it easier for agents to skip when evidence is sparse rather than explicitly acknowledging the absence.
recommendations.fix: Make Section K mandatory for all outputs: either include verified citations with DOI, OR produce an explicit statement: 'No verified formal citations were identified for this topic at the required confidence level. Key mechanism claims are based on training knowledge and should be verified with current PubMed/Embase search.'

recommendations.title: Hard Rule 8 boundary between 'weak link' and 'research gap' is ambiguous for adversarial inputs
recommendations.problem: When users explicitly request gap analysis alongside mechanism mapping, the distinction between 'weak link' (allowed in Section H) and 'research gap' (requires dedicated gap-analysis step) can collapse — skill may partially frame weak links as research gaps without explicitly declining the gap-declaration request.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard Rule 8 prohibits converting weak links to formal gaps but does not define explicit language for declining a combined map+gap request.
recommendations.fix: Add a note to Hard Rule 8: 'If the user requests both mechanism mapping and gap analysis in one prompt, complete the mechanism map first, then explicitly state: Gap analysis requires a separate step using the dedicated gap-finder skill. Use the Section I hypothesis entry points and Section J routing as your starting point.'

recommendations.title: Description lacks colloquial trigger phrases for diverse mapping scenarios
recommendations.problem: Description accurately describes formal structure but omits natural user phrases that would trigger this skill ('how does this disease work', 'mechanism overview', 'before I decide what to study').
recommendations.root_cause: Description written for content accuracy rather than triggering optimization.
recommendations.fix: Add trigger phrases to description or Sample Triggers: 'mechanism evidence map', 'how this disease works at the molecular level', 'disease mechanism architecture before study design', 'evidence chain for this pathway'.
